name: TAO 4h Failed Breakdown Hold v1
edge_mechanism: a breakdown fails, price reclaims structure, then must hold that reclaim before continuation into the unwind leg is allowed
thesis: The cycle explicitly calls for a failed-breakdown reclaim that must hold before entry. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it has more structural amplitude than ETH and more transition potential than BTC chop, so a reclaim hold can lead to a meaningful unwind leg rather than a flat bounce.
invalidation_condition: If failed breakdowns are still too sparse, or if reclaim holds repeatedly fail before the unwind can develop, the mechanism is invalid.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below a recent boundary, reclaims it quickly, proves that reclaim on the next bar, and then continues higher as the failed downside move unwinds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Failed downside auctions on expressive 4h lanes can create sharp unwind moves, but only when the reclaim is real. The hold requirement separates absorption from random bounce noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because the lane has enough amplitude for a reclaim-hold sequence to generate tradable continuation, unlike flatter chop lanes where the same idea may stay too small.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: TAO may reclaim quickly but fail to hold, meaning continuation entries come into exhaustion rather than real absorption.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If it trades but overextends too little after entry, move more profit to the first scale-out before altering the reclaim hold sequence.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A smaller set of sharper unwind moves, with early partials contributing most and occasional runners when the failed break truly reverses.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h generates enough failed-breakdown holds to clear screen density and reaches early targets often enough to validate the unwind thesis.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or reclaim-hold entries that fail before the first target too often.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but still denser than prior failed-break repair families because the rules are simpler and role-separated

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for failed-breakdown unwind continuation after reclaim hold.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough reclaim-hold events and first-target behavior clears costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h reclaim holds remain too sparse or unstable.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count than chop reversion ideas, but larger unwind legs once the hold confirms.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same failed-breakdown hold logic survives on the cycle primary lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h preserves the reclaim-hold pattern with cleaner but smaller unwinds.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h is too choppy and shallow for the mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, cleaner reclaim holds than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same failed-breakdown hold logic works on a cleaner major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still shows reclaim-hold continuation with enough density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the idea is too sparse outside TAO amplitude.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sparser but cleaner unwind structure than TAO.

long: Enter only if next bar low stays >= DONCHIAN_LOW_20 - 0.05 * ATR_14 and close remains above EMA_20
short: Enter only if next bar high stays <= DONCHIAN_HIGH_20 + 0.05 * ATR_14 and close remains below EMA_20

long: Exit remaining 30% on loss of EMA_20 or touch of EMA_55
short: Exit remaining 30% on regain of EMA_20 or touch of EMA_55
